title: alyssum harputicum
intro: Alyssum harputicum, also known as Turkish Alyssum, is a perennial herb belonging to the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its small, white, or purple flowers and feathery leaves. Traditionally, Alyssum harputicum has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which help to reduce pain and swelling, and prevent infection. The health benefits of Alyssum harputicum include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its ability to support the immune system and reduce stress. In herbal preparations, Alyssum harputicum is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be applied topically to wounds and skin irritations.
